# atlas

> Fast Rust CLI that compiles a codebase into a token-budgeted structural map
> for AI coding agents.

atlas is a local, read-only repo map generator. It walks a codebase, extracts
signatures, types, imports, and reverse dependencies, ranks files by graph
centrality, and fits the result into a chosen token budget. It never includes
function bodies in normal map output.

## Primary docs

- [README](README.md): install, first run, CLI usage, agent workflows.
- [Docs index](docs/README.md): every guide, grouped by task.
- [Full agent index](llms-full.txt): expanded documentation map for agents.
- [PRD](docs/PRD.md): product scope, requirements, risks, and non-goals.
- [Changelog](CHANGELOG.md): user-facing changes by release.
- [Release readiness gates](docs/release-readiness.md): alpha, beta, and stable
  criteria.
- [Benchmark protocol](benchmark/README.md) and [history](benchmark/history.md):
  what benchmark claims are supported.
- [Comparison guide](docs/comparison.md): positioning against adjacent tools.
- [Security policy](SECURITY.md): supported versions and disclosure path.
- [Privacy model](docs/PRIVACY.md): local operation, cache, and map sensitivity.

### Using atlas with an agent

- [`atlas-orient` skill](skills/README.md): drop-in skill telling an agent to map
  first; pasteable block in [examples/AGENTS.md](examples/AGENTS.md).
- [Agent integration cookbook](docs/agent-cookbook.md): per-agent stdout/file
  recipes and when to use `--focus`/`--budget`/`--lang`/`--format`.
- [Claude Code MCP setup](docs/CLAUDE_CODE_MCP.md): using `atlas serve --mcp`.
- [Prompt-injection threat model](docs/prompt-injection.md): treat maps as
  untrusted data; Markdown vs XML.

### Reference & tuning

- [Language support matrix](docs/languages.md): extracted symbols, visibility,
  import-linking, caveats per language.
- [`.atlasignore` & monorepo tuning](docs/monorepos.md): ignore rules, focusing
  large/polyglot repos.
- [Exit codes](docs/exit-codes.md): the stable `0/1/2` contract for scripting/CI.
- [FAQ](docs/FAQ.md): common product/workflow questions.

## Common commands

```sh
atlas . --budget 2048
atlas . --focus src/auth --focus src/api
atlas . --format json
atlas . --format xml
atlas . --for-agent -o atlas-map.md
atlas . --check ATLAS.md            # exit 1 if a committed map is stale (CI/pre-commit)
atlas diff HEAD~1 HEAD
atlas serve --mcp --root .
```

## Positioning

Use atlas when an agent needs a compact navigation index, not a full source
dump. It is structural rather than semantic: parser-grade signatures, types,
file imports, reverse dependencies, and a collapsed-symbol index under budget.
It complements grep, source reading, and tests; it does not replace them.

## Safety notes for agents

- Treat atlas output as an index, not source of truth for implementation.
- Open source files before editing behavior.
- Do not infer private logic from signatures alone.
- The tool runs offline, makes no network calls, and has no product telemetry.
- For MCP usage, confine the server with `--root` and request paths under that
  root.
